summary: First-time contributor PR using objdiff and `python tools/decomp.py`. The merge kept the `lbmemory` work: added a decompilation/prototype for `fn_80015184`, an alarm-driven chunked memory-copy callback working through a local `LBMgr` struct at `g_alloc + 0x6A0`. The slice also adds address comments to `hsd_3B34.h`, but reviewer noted the `hsd_3B34` functions had already been submitted with higher match scores in PR #2488. decomp.dev reported `main/melee/lb/lbmemory::fn_80015184` improving from 0.00% to 88.61%, with a small `.sdata` improvement and `.data` regression.

key_files.reason: Adds static assert strings, local `struct LBMgr`, and `fn_80015184` implementation.
Includes `<dolphin/os/OSAlarm.h>` and exposes `fn_80015184(OSAlarm*, OSContext*)` with address comment.
Adds address comments to existing `hsd_803B....` prototypes; related function work was superseded by PR #2488 per review.

what_changed: Added `fn_80015184(OSAlarm* alarm, OSContext* context)` to `lbmemory.c` and `lbmemory.h`.
Introduced a local `struct LBMgr` layout with `OSAlarm alarm` at offset 0x00, `src` at 0x28, `dst` at 0x2C, `size` at 0x30, `offset` at 0x34, `cb_arg` at 0x38, and callback pointer at 0x3C.

The new function obtains the manager with `(struct LBMgr*) ((u8*) &g_alloc + 0x6A0)`, asserts `p->size` is nonzero, copies up to `0x19000` bytes from `src + offset` to `dst + offset`, advances `offset`, invokes `cb(0, cb_arg, 0, 0)` when complete, otherwise recreates/sets an OS alarm.
Added static strings `lbl_803BA2C0[0xB] = "lbmemory.c"` and `lbMemory_804D3794[8] = "p->size"` near the function, although the function body still uses string literals in the diff excerpt.
Added address-prefix comments such as `/* 3B3408 */` to declarations in `hsd_3B34.h`.
decomp.dev bot reported improvements for `fn_80015184`, `.sdata`, and several `hsd_3B34` items, but also a small `main/melee/lb/lbmemory .data` regression.
smart_moves: Recovered a concrete manager struct from observed offsets instead of leaving all access as raw pointer arithmetic.
Matched the callback state machine with a clear chunk-size clamp: remaining bytes are capped at `0x19000` before `memcpy`.
Kept the decompiled function under the unresolved-address naming style `fn_80015184`, appropriate when a semantic name is not yet known.
Added the correct public header dependency for the exposed OS alarm callback signature.
Used address comments in headers, matching common project practice for generated/decompiled symbol tracking.
decomp_lessons: When a global allocator or blob field is only known by offset, a small local struct with verified field offsets can make a partial decomp readable while preserving match behavior.
For alarm/timer callbacks, expose the function with the platform callback types (`OSAlarm*`, `OSContext*`) once the signature is known.
Assert strings and file-name strings may affect `.sdata`/`.data`; adding or relocating them can improve one section while regressing another, so check section-level bot reports.
Use objdiff and `python tools/decomp.py` to choose tractable functions, but coordinate with open/nearby PRs because overlapping functions can be superseded by higher-score submissions.
Header-only cleanup such as address comments is useful, but it should be separated mentally from actual function matching progress when reviewing reports.
naming_conventions: `lbMemory_800150F0`, `lbMemory_8001529C`, etc. use the subsystem prefix plus address style for known lbmemory symbols.
`fn_80015184` is used for an unresolved function at address 0x80015184.
`hsd_803B3408` style is retained for baselib functions whose semantic names are not known.
Header declarations use address comments of the form `/* 015184 */` or `/* 3B3408 */` before prototypes.

Auto-generated/local temporary variable names such as `temp_r3`, `temp_r3_2`, `temp_r6`, and `var_r30` were left in the decompiled function, indicating the function was still match-oriented rather than fully cleaned up.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Casts `&g_alloc` to `u8*` before adding `0x6A0`, a common tactic for matching byte-offset access into a global allocation/control area.
Uses unsigned constants with `U` suffixes in comparisons and assignments, e.g. `0U`, `0x127U`, and `0x19000U`, to steer codegen toward unsigned compare/subtract behavior.
Implements `min(size - offset, 0x19000)` manually with a temporary and conditional assignment, likely reflecting the target branch structure.
Keeps the callback invocation exactly as four `u32`-like arguments: `cb(0, cb_arg, 0, 0)`.
Recreates and resets an `OSAlarm` through `OSCreateAlarm(&temp_r3->alarm)` and `OSSetAlarm(&temp_r3->alarm, 0x10624DD3, NULL)` when more copy work remains.

review_feedback.feedback: Thanks for the contribution; `hsd_3B34` functions were already submitted with higher match scores in #2488, but `lbmemory` could still be merged.
`main/melee/lb/lbmemory::fn_80015184` improved from 0.00% to 88.61%; `main/melee/lb/lbmemory .sdata` improved from 66.67% to 83.33%; `main/melee/lb/lbmemory .data` regressed from 93.33% to 92.18%.

follow_up_queries: Compare PR #2488 for the higher-match `hsd_3B34` submissions referenced by the reviewer.
Look for later renames or semantic identification of `fn_80015184` and `struct LBMgr`.
Check whether `lbl_803BA2C0` and `lbMemory_804D3794` were later deduplicated or adjusted to fix the small `.data` regression.
Search for other users of `g_alloc + 0x6A0` to confirm the manager struct ownership and improve its header placement.
